name: StyleX (Meta's Atomic CSS-in-JS)

performance.notes: Compile-time atomic CSS — smallest possible CSS bundle

agent_readiness.notes: Meta's atomic CSS-in-JS used at facebook.com and instagram.com scale. stylex.create({base: {color: 'red'}}). stylex.props(styles.base). Compile-time — zero runtime CSS injection. Atomic CSS: each rule generates one class. Deterministic specificity. Type-safe CSS tokens. Babel/webpack/vite plugin required. Works with React, Preact, plain HTML. Designed for massive scale with predictable styling. Alternative to Tailwind with type-safety.

gotchas: Verbose API compared to Tailwind or emotion
Still relatively new — ecosystem smaller than Tailwind
Dynamic styles require specific patterns (stylex.create not called dynamically)
best_when: Large-scale React applications where CSS bundle size critical
Teams wanting type-safe, deterministic CSS without class conflicts
Component libraries needing predictable style composition
avoid_when: Small projects (overhead of setup not worth it)
Teams already invested in Tailwind CSS
Dynamic styling requiring runtime CSS generation
summary: StyleX is Meta's atomic CSS-in-JS library used at instagram.com and facebook.com scale. stylex.create({styles}) + stylex.props(). Compile-time — generates atomic CSS classes at build time (zero runtime). Deterministic specificity, type-safe tokens, collision-free. Babel/webpack plugin required. Trade-off: more verbose than Tailwind but type-safe and deterministic. For large-scale React apps.
